text: Large language models, or LLMs, are advanced computer programs that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by analyzing patterns in vast amounts of text data, learning from books, articles, and websites. This training helps them predict the next word in a sentence or create coherent responses. To use an LLM, people often apply prompt engineering—carefully crafting input questions or instructions to get better results. LLMs are capable of tasks like writing essays, summarizing information, or translating languages. However, they have limits: they may produce incorrect or biased information, lack true understanding, and cannot think creatively or form real opinions.
